facts.text: Sicklerville grew around a 1872 railroad stop called Sicklertown, the first station on the Philadelphia & Atlantic City line’s Williamstown Branch.
A Methodist church founded in 1837 on land donated by William Sickler doubled as a school and later became the town’s first cemetery in the 1840s.
Sicklerville’s post office opened in 1874 and still sits near its original 19th‑century location on Sicklerville Road.
The area that is now Sicklerville was first cleared for charcoal to fuel Philadelphia’s iron industry, thanks to dense pine and cedar forests in the early 1800s.
In 1845, the region left Gloucester Township and joined the newly formed Winslow Township, a change formalized by the New Jersey Legislature.
